threat_model: Durable Gmail runtime access touches OAuth authority, user mailbox metadata, connector tokens, refresh-token persistence, external Google endpoints, and approval-gated write operations. The primary hazards are overbroad scopes, token disclosure, unverified sensitive or restricted scope use, refresh-token persistence without revocation, and treating a read-only probe token as approval for send or draft actions.

required_checks.check: redaction test for access tokens, refresh tokens, client secrets, and private keys
policy bypass test for provider-side mutation and write-action approval gates
receipt integrity test for UAO, causal trace, and security receipt refs
audit visibility test for durable OAuth evidence gates and blocked release state

findings.mitigation: Complete provider-side OAuth consent, client, scope, refresh-token, revocation, rotation, and failed-refresh witnesses under explicit operator authority before any durable production runtime claim.

mitigations: block Google Cloud credential creation in this repository-local change
require least-privilege Gmail scope selection before OAuth client mutation
require redacted secret presence receipts instead of token value serialization
require revocation, rotation, expiration, and failed-refresh tests before durable readiness
keep Gmail send and draft actions approval-gated
